name: Fibronectin reconstitution restored aged muscle regeneration in mice
summary: Reconstituting fibronectin levels in the aged skeletal-muscle stem-cell niche remobilized muscle stem cells and restored youth-like regeneration in mice.
statement: Aged mouse skeletal-muscle niches had reduced fibronectin, and reconstituting fibronectin levels remobilized muscle stem cells and restored youth-like muscle regeneration.
population_or_model: Aged mouse skeletal-muscle stem-cell niche and regeneration models
time_horizon: Post-injury regeneration after extracellular-matrix niche manipulation
quantitative_note: This finding is staged as direct animal niche-reconstitution evidence.
caveats: The evidence is tissue-specific to skeletal muscle in mice.
Fibronectin delivery, localization, dose, and durability remain translational obstacles.
Restoring one extracellular-matrix component may not generalize across aged stem-cell niches.
